text: Backtracking Pattern: Core template. Build solution incrementally. At each step, try all valid choices. If choice leads to invalid state or dead end: undo choice (backtrack) and try next. Template: def bt(state): if goal(state): record; return; for choice in valid_choices(state): apply(choice); bt(state); undo(choice). Key: explicit undo step restores state. Time: typically exponential.

Backtracking Pattern: Permutations. Generate all orderings of n elements. State: current permutation, remaining elements. At each step, try each remaining element. Template: def bt(path, remaining): if not remaining: result.append(path[:]); return; for i,v in enumerate(remaining): path.append(v); bt(path, remaining[:i]+remaining[i+1:]); path.pop(). Time: O(n! * n). For duplicates: sort and skip same-value elements.

Backtracking Pattern: Subsets / power set. Generate all 2^n subsets. Two approaches: 1) For each element, decide include/exclude. 2) At each step, extend current subset with any remaining element. Template: def bt(index, current): result.append(current[:]); for i in range(index, n): current.append(nums[i]); bt(i+1, current); current.pop(). Time: O(2^n * n).

Backtracking Pattern: Combination sum. Find combinations summing to target; elements can repeat. Sort candidates. Prune: skip if candidate > remaining. Allow same index for reuse. Template: def bt(start, remaining, path): if remaining==0: record; return; for i in range(start, len(candidates)): if candidates[i] > remaining: break; path.append(candidates[i]); bt(i, remaining-candidates[i], path); path.pop(). Time: O(2^(t/min)).

Backtracking Pattern: N-Queens. Place N non-attacking queens on NxN board. State: set of used columns, diagonals (r-c), anti-diagonals (r+c). For each row, try each column. Skip if column or diagonal conflicts. Template: def bt(row): if row==n: record; return; for col in range(n): if col not in cols and (row-col) not in diag and (row+col) not in anti_diag: place and recurse. Time: O(n!).

Backtracking Pattern: Sudoku solver. For each empty cell, try digits 1-9. Check row, column, 3x3 box validity. If valid, place and recurse. If recursion fails, remove digit and try next. Base case: no empty cells = solved. Time: O(9^m) where m=empty cells. Optimization: try cell with fewest valid choices first (forward checking / MRV heuristic).

Backtracking Pattern: Word search in grid. DFS backtracking from each cell. Match characters in sequence. Mark cell visited temporarily (modify grid or use visited set). Backtrack by unmarking. Four-directional movement. Template: def bt(row, col, idx): if idx==len(word): return True; if out_of_bounds or visited or grid[row][col]!=word[idx]: return False; mark; try all 4 dirs; unmark; return False. Time: O(m*n*4^L).

Backtracking Pattern: Generate valid parentheses. State: current string, open count, close count. Add '(' if open < n. Add ')' if close < open. Recurse. Base case: length == 2n. Template: def bt(s, open, close): if len(s)==2*n: result.append(s); return; if open<n: bt(s+'(', open+1, close); if close<open: bt(s+')', open, close+1). Time: O(4^n / sqrt(n)) — Catalan number.

Backtracking Pattern: Letter combinations of phone number. Map digit to letters (2->abc, 3->def, ...). For each digit, try each corresponding letter. Recurse with next digit. Template: def bt(index, path): if index==len(digits): result.append(path); return; for letter in mapping[digits[index]]: bt(index+1, path+letter). Time: O(4^n * n) where n=digits length, 4=max letters per digit.

Backtracking Pattern: Palindrome partitioning. Partition string into all-palindrome substrings. Backtrack: at each position, try all palindrome substrings starting there. Template: def bt(start, path): if start==len(s): record; return; for end in range(start+1, len(s)+1): if is_palindrome(s[start:end]): path.append(s[start:end]); bt(end, path); path.pop(). Optimize with DP palindrome precomputation. Time: O(n * 2^n).

Backtracking Pattern: Expression add operators. Add +, -, * between digits to reach target. Track current value and last multiplied value (for * precedence). Template: def bt(index, path, cur_val, last_mul): if index==len(num) and cur_val==target: record. Try each prefix as operand. +: bt(next, path+'+'+str(n), cur_val+n, n). -: bt(next, ..., cur_val-n, -n). *: bt(next, ..., cur_val-last_mul+last_mul*n, last_mul*n). Time: O(4^n * n).

Backtracking Pattern: Coloring problems (graph coloring). Assign colors to nodes such that adjacent nodes have different colors. State: assignment of colors to nodes so far. For each uncolored node, try each color. Check if color conflicts with neighbors. Backtrack if all colors conflict. K-coloring is NP-complete in general; backtracking is exact but exponential. Time: O(k^n) worst case.

Backtracking Pattern: Pruning strategies. 1) Feasibility pruning: if current state can't possibly lead to solution, abandon. 2) Bound pruning: if current partial solution is already worse than best found, abandon. 3) Symmetry breaking: avoid exploring symmetric states. 4) Forward checking: precompute which values are still valid. Good pruning transforms exponential to practical. Example: in combination sum, sort and break early.

Backtracking Pattern: Unique permutations (with duplicates). Sort array. Use 'used' boolean array. Skip duplicate values: if nums[i]==nums[i-1] and not used[i-1], skip. This ensures duplicates are used in fixed order. Template: def bt(path, used): if len(path)==n: record; for i in range(n): if used[i]: continue; if i>0 and nums[i]==nums[i-1] and not used[i-1]: continue; used[i]=True; recurse; used[i]=False. Time: O(n!).

Backtracking Pattern: Rat in a maze / path finding. Find all paths from (0,0) to (n-1,n-1). Move in valid directions (not blocked, not visited). Backtrack when stuck. Template: def bt(row, col, path): if (row,col)==(n-1,n-1): record; return; for dr,dc in directions: nr,nc = row+dr,col+dc; if valid(nr,nc) and not visited[nr][nc]: visited[nr][nc]=True; bt(nr,nc,path); visited[nr][nc]=False. Time: O(4^(m*n)).

Backtracking Pattern: Restore IP addresses. Insert dots to form valid IP. Exactly 4 parts, each 0-255, no leading zeros. Backtrack with state: current position in string, parts found so far. For each part length (1,2,3 chars), check if valid number. Template: def bt(start, parts): if len(parts)==4 and start==len(s): record; return; for length in 1,2,3: segment=s[start:start+length]; if valid(segment): bt(start+length, parts+[segment]). Time: O(27) = O(1).

Backtracking Pattern: Backtracking vs DP. Use backtracking when: need all solutions (not just count/optimal), state space is small, optimal substructure doesn't hold cleanly. Use DP when: only need count or optimal value, overlapping subproblems exist. Example: count paths in grid → DP. Find all paths → backtracking. Combination sum count → DP. Find all combinations → backtracking.

Backtracking Pattern: Knight's tour. Visit all squares of chessboard using knight moves exactly once. Backtrack: place knight, try all 8 L-shaped moves, recurse. Mark visited squares. Warnsdorff's heuristic: always move to square with fewest onward moves (greedy, reduces backtracking dramatically). Time: O(8^(n²)) without heuristic, near O(n²) with Warnsdorff.

Backtracking Pattern: Bitmasking in backtracking. Track used elements with bitmask instead of boolean array or set. Faster set operations and compact state representation. Example: permutations with bitmask. def bt(mask, path): if mask==(1<<n)-1: record; for i in range(n): if not (mask>>i)&1: bt(mask|(1<<i), path+[nums[i]]). Also useful for memoizing states in bitmask DP when subproblems repeat.

Backtracking Pattern: Remove invalid parentheses. Find all minimum-removal valid parentheses strings. Count minimum removals needed (count unmatched brackets). Backtrack: at each position, keep or remove bracket. Prune: if removals_used > min_removals. Check validity at end. Remove duplicates with set. Time: O(2^n) but heavily pruned in practice. BFS approach also valid (shorter code, same complexity).
